Items.Title: 笑话日基金会第 #2 部分 | Jokeday Funday Part #2
Items.Description:
笑话 1：调试器的困境

为什么程序员喜欢在黑暗中工作？

因为光线有时会让它们的虫子更难被发现！

笑话 2：茶歇

为什么程序员更喜欢喝咖啡？

因为它可以帮助他们用 Java 语言编写代码！

笑话 3：代码诗人

当代码拒绝编译时，开发人员对他们的代码说了些什么？

笑话 4：有条件的喜剧

开发商为何破产？

因为他们在 if-else 循环中丢失了条件语句！

笑话 5：键盘难题

为什么键盘不高兴？

因为它的钥匙太多了，却没有人能找到它的逃脱！

笑话 6：编程海盗

海盗最喜欢的编程语言是什么？

罗！

笑话 7：技术支持的扭曲

技术支持人员为什么去钓鱼？

因为他们想捕获一些字节！

笑话 8：递归之谜

为什么程序员总是把圣诞节和万圣节搞混了？

因为 10 月 31 日 == 12 月 25 日，但递归是没有界限的！

笑话 9：CSS 派对常客

为什么样式表从来没有出现在聚会上？

因为它被卡在了选择器中！

笑话 10：算法的迷恋

算法为什么要和数据库约会呢？

因为他们的化学反应很好，总能找到共同的兴趣！

奖金笑话：作者的特别

因为他想将自己的编码能力转化为超能力，调试世界！



原文:

Joke 1: The Debugger's Dilemma

Why do programmers prefer to work in the dark?

Because the light can sometimes make their bugs more difficult to spot!

Joke 2: The Coffee Break

Why do programmers prefer to drink coffee?

Because it helps them Java-nate their code!

Joke 3: The Code Poet

What did the developer say to their code when it refused to compile?

"Don't be so codependent!"

Joke 4: The Conditional Comedy

Why did the developer go broke?

Because they lost their conditional statement in a if-else loop!

Joke 5: The Keyboard Conundrum

Why was the keyboard not happy?

Because it had too many keys, but no one could find its escape!

Joke 6: The Programming Pirate

What's a pirate's favorite programming language?

R!

Joke 7: The Tech Support Twist

Why did the tech support person go fishing?

Because they wanted to catch some bytes!

Joke 8: The Recursive Riddle

Why do programmers always get Christmas and Halloween confused?

Because Oct 31 == Dec 25, but recursion knows no bounds!

Joke 9: The CSS Partygoer

Why did the style sheet never make it to the party?

Because it was stuck in a selector!

Joke 10: The Algorithm's Crush

Why did the algorithm go on a date with the database?

Because they had great chemistry and could always find common interests!

Bonus Joke: The Author's Special

Why did Soumyadeep become a programmer?

Because he wanted to turn his coding skills into a superpower and debug the world!

Items.Title: 笑话日基金会第 #1 部分 | Jokeday Funday Part #1
Items.Description:
笑话 1：无限循环

为什么程序员总是混淆圣诞节和万圣节？

因为10月31日==12月25日！

笑话 2：时间旅行错误

程序员为何破产？

因为他在时空循环中失去了领域！

笑话 3：二元吟游诗人

世界上只有10种人：

那些懂二进制的人，那些不懂二进制的人，还有那些没想到这个笑话是 3 进制的人！

笑话 4：懒惰的开发人员

为什么程序员更喜欢深色模式？

因为光会吸引虫子！

笑话 5：深夜编码员

为什么程序员总是搞混左右呢？

因为他们总是使用指针来工作！

笑话 6：HTML 爱好者

为什么网络开发人员总是带着铅笔和纸？

万一他们需要画一个标签！

笑话7：多变的变色龙

为什么变量与函数分开了？

听说该函数返回 null！

笑话 8：CSS 派对

为什么样式表独自去参加聚会？

因为没有课！

笑话 9：JavaScript 侦探

为什么开发商破产了还要雇侦探？

寻找他们代码中的错误！

笑话 10：算法单口会

为什么程序员不喜欢单口喜剧？

因为他们的笑话总是陷入调试的无限循环！

奖金笑话：作者的特别

因为他要把自己的想法变成字节大小的现实！



原文:

Joke 1: The Infinite Loop

Why do programmers always mix up Christmas and Halloween?

Because Oct 31 == Dec 25!

Joke 2: The Time Travel Bug

Why did the programmer go broke?

Because he lost his domain in a time travel loop!

Joke 3: The Binary Bard

There are only 10 types of people in the world:

Those who understand binary, those who don't, and those who didn't expect this joke to be in base 3!

Joke 4: The Lazy Developer

Why do programmers prefer dark mode?

Because light attracts bugs!

Joke 5: The Late-Night Coder

Why do programmers always mix up their left and right?

Because they are always working with pointers!

Joke 6: The HTML Lover

Why did the web developer always carry a pencil and paper?

In case they needed to draw a tag!

Joke 7: The Variable Chameleon

Why did the variable break up with the function?

It heard the function was returning null!

Joke 8: The CSS Party

Why did the stylesheet go to the party alone?

Because it had no class!

Joke 9: The JavaScript Detective

Why did the developer go broke hiring a detective?

To find a bug in their code!

Joke 10: The Algorithm Stand-Up

Why don't programmers like stand-up comedy?

Because their jokes always get stuck in an infinite loop of debugging!

Bonus Joke: The Author's Special

Why did Soumyadeep become a programmer?

Because he wanted to turn his ideas into byte-sized reality!

原文:

Welcome to the world of Python programming! Python is a versatile and beginner-friendly language that's widely used for web development, data analysis, artificial intelligence, and more. This guide will help you take your first steps into the Python ecosystem.

Installing Python

To start coding in Python, you'll need to install it on your machine. Visit the official Python website and download the latest version of Python for your operating system.

Useful Links:

  • Official Python Website
  • Installation Guide by Real Python

Python Basics

Python syntax is known for its readability and simplicity. Here are some key concepts to get you started:

  • Variables and Data Types: In Python, you can declare variables without specifying their types. Python has various built-in data types, including integers, floats, strings, lists, dictionaries, and more.
  • Control Flow: Python provides conditional statements like if-else and loops like for and while to control the flow of your program.
  • Functions and Modules: You can define functions to reuse code and organize it into modules.

Useful Links:

Getting Help and Community Support

Python has a vast and supportive community. If you run into any issues or have questions, here are some resources to seek help:

  • Stack Overflow: A popular Q&A website where you can find answers to programming questions.
  • r/learnpython: The Python community on Reddit, where you can ask for help and participate in discussions.
  • Official Python Discourse: The official forum for Python users to seek help and discuss various topics.

Useful Links:

  • Stack Overflow Python Tag
  • r/learnpython on Reddit
  • Official Python Discourse

Conclusion

Congratulations on taking your first steps into the world of Python programming! We've covered the basics of installing Python, key language concepts, and where to find help when you need it.

Remember, practice is essential when learning any programming language. Keep experimenting, building projects, and exploring the rich Python ecosystem. Happy coding!

Items.Title: 真实 DOM、虚拟 DOM、影子 DOM，有什么区别？ | Real DOM, Virtual DOM, Shadow DOM, What's the Difference?
Items.Description:
琳西·凯·威廉姆斯

发表于 7 月 16 日

真实 DOM、虚拟 DOM 和影子 DOM，天哪！ 让我们深入了解它们如何协同工作来创建一个干净、高性能的文档对象模型。

网站的 HTML 树由一个名为document的对象表示。 在这个对象中，有一个 HTML 树元素的模型，可以通过对象点表示法方便地访问。

  • 您还可以使用此点表示法来操作 DOM。 例如： document.body.style.background = 'red'会将正文的背景颜色更改为红色。 您可以在Mozilla 的网络文档中查看有关 DOM API 的更多信息。

这个 DOM 被称为页面的真实 DOM 。 真正的 DOM 本身只能在每次 DOM 发生更改时同时更新整个 DOM。 这使得页面更新变得非常缓慢且昂贵。 这就是虚拟 DOM 来拯救世界的地方！

虚拟 DOM是真实 DOM 的虚拟表示。 该虚拟 DOM 保存在内存中并与真实 DOM 同步。 然后，虚拟 DOM 制作其自身的副本（我们将其称为虚拟 DOM 2）。 当页面上进行更新时，它首先应用于虚拟 DOM 2。React 然后将虚拟 DOM 2 与原始虚拟 DOM（真实 DOM 的精确副本）进行比较。 这就是神奇的地方，只更新需要更新的内容会更快、更便宜。

如果您想查看虚拟 DOM 的运行情况，您可以在 Google Chrome 的检查工具中通过“Paint flashing”功能查看可视化表示：

选中该框，然后使用该页面，任何 DOM 更改都将用绿色框突出显示。

最后但并非最不重要的一点是，我们有难以捉摸的影子 DOM 。 这允许隐藏的 DOM 树附加到常规 DOM 树中的元素。 可以使用Web API创建自定义元素，这些元素仅由影子 DOM 控制。 重要的是要记住，真实 DOM 和影子 DOM 是完全不同的领域。 对真实 DOM 中的元素所做的更改不会应用于影子 DOM 中的元素，反之亦然。

  • 如果您在检查工具中调查 HTML 元素，并且在页面上正确查看时没有看到列出的内容，那么它可能位于影子 DOM 中。 最好的例子是视频播放器。 在页面上，您可以直观地看到内部 UI：播放按钮、跳过按钮、共享按钮等，但在真实 DOM 中看不到它。 它由 Shadow DOM 控制。

尽管它可能难以捉摸，但如果您需要的话，您实际上可以显示影子 DOM！ 在 Google Chrome 的检查工具中：转到设置，选择首选项，然后转到元素部分。 选择“显示用户代理影子 DOM”以在检查工具的“元素”选项卡中与真实 DOM 一起显示任何影子 DOM。

这就是全部三个！ 希望这篇博客能让您更好地理解真实 DOM、虚拟 DOM 和影子 DOM 之间的差异。 请在评论中告诉我您的想法！

原文:

Lyndsi Kay Williams

Posted on Jul 16

 

Real DOMs and virtual DOMs and shadow DOMs, oh my! Let's take a dive to see how they all work together to create a clean, performant Document Object Model.

The DOM (Document Object Model) is exactly as it states. The HTML tree of a website is represented by an object called document. In this object there is a model of the HTML tree's elements, conveniently accessible by object dot notation.

  • document.head will return the <head></head> section of the tree, document.body will return the <body></body> section of the tree, and so on. You can also use this dot notation to manipulate the DOM. For example: document.body.style.background = ‘red’ will change the body’s background color to red. You can see more about the DOM API in Mozilla's web docs.

This DOM is referred to as the page's real DOM. The real DOM, by itself, is only able to update the entire DOM simultaneously every time there is a change to the DOM. This makes it very slow and expensive to make updates to the page. That's where the virtual DOM comes to save the day!

The virtual DOM is a virtual representation of the real DOM. This virtual DOM is kept in memory and synced with the real DOM. React compiles the real DOM into Javascript, which is the first step in creating more performant updates. The virtual DOM then makes a copy of itself (let's call it virtual DOM 2). When an update is made on the page, it is first applied to virtual DOM 2. React then compares virtual DOM 2 to the original virtual DOM, an exact copy of the real DOM. React uses this comparison to quickly detect where the real DOM needs to be updated and updates only those elements instead of the entire DOM. This is where the magic happens, it is much quicker and less expensive to update only what needs to be updated.

If you want to see the virtual DOM in action, you can see a visual representation with the "Paint flashing" feature in Google Chrome's inspect tool:

Check that box and then play with the page, any DOM changes will be highlighted with a green box.

Last but not least, we have the elusive shadow DOM. This allows hidden DOM trees to be attached to elements in the regular DOM tree. Custom elements can be created with the Web API, these are controlled solely by the shadow DOM. It's important to remember that the real DOM and the shadow DOM are completely different realms. Changes made to elements in the real DOM will not apply to elements in the shadow DOM, and vice versa.

  • If you're sleuthing through HTML elements in the inspect tool and don't see something listed when you're looking right at it on the page, it's probably in the shadow DOM. The best example is video players. Visually on the page you can see the internal UI: the play button, skip button, share button, etc. but you don't see it in the real DOM. It's being controlled by the shadow DOM.

Elusive as it may be, you can actually reveal the shadow DOM if you need it! In Google Chrome's inspect tool: Go to the settings, select Preferences and go to the Elements section. Select "Show user agent shadow DOM" to show any shadow DOMs alongside the real DOM in the Elements tab of the inspect tool.

That's all three! Hopefully this blog has brought you a better understanding of the differences among the real DOM, the virtual DOM, and the shadow DOM. Let me know what you think in the comments!

Shivam Chaurasia - Jun 19

Items.Title: 这可能是您的下一个项目 | This can be your next project
Items.Description:
什雷·维杰瓦尔吉亚

发表于 7 月 16 日

开发它，分享它，找到下一份工作，然后感谢我

引擎盖下

我不会说太多，但你读过我上一篇 LinkedIn 帖子了吗？

道理很简单，我已经跟很多开发者讲过很多次了，如何去抢工作，如何学习编程，如何成为顶尖的开发者等等等等。

问题很简单，我们是否需要学习 DSA，我们是否需要掌握一些顶级的 Udemy 课程，或者我们是否需要通过 Chat GPT 制作简历才能脱颖而出？

编写程序来学习编程

这不再是火箭科学，我们需要编写程序来学习编程。

我们需要开发项目才能成为优秀的开发人员甚至经验丰富的开发人员。

经验丰富的开发人员之所以经验丰富，并不是因为年龄，而是因为他们在开发各种项目方面拥有丰富的经验。

或者经验丰富的开发人员是那些能够一次开发一件事但几乎达到完美主义水平的人。

所以即使Chat GPT在未来几年到来，你也别无选择，开发人员仍然必须编写程序才能成为优秀的开发人员。

启动小项目

因此，既然我们必须了解要做什么，那么现在是时候知道从哪里开始了。

我给出了一个项目想法，即现实世界的网站项目，这是一个全栈项目，因此您可以选择要选择的技术堆栈。

在印度，80-90%的交易是通过称为UPI的在线支付完成的，主要由中产阶级和以上阶层人士完成。

该模式尚未在其他国家推出，但印度遥遥领先，法国和日本等其他国家也在效仿相同的模式。

网上交易简单有效且不易出错。

现在，由于大部分交易都是在线进行的，人们可以轻松管理费用。

该项目旨在开发

在线谷歌支付费用管理器

  • 用户将通过网站上的电话号码登录以连接google pay
  • 用户可以查看最近 100 或 200 或 3/4 个月的交易。
  • 网站应对交易进行分类，以便用户轻松了解他/她的所有费用
  • 可以提供更多过滤器选项以及实时图表，以便更好地进行分析。 简单而有效的项目，尽量让它好看。

我的建议是使用简单的黑白组合来使其有吸引力。

请参阅此示例设计
样品设计

行动计划

  • 我们需要 Google API 来获取用户交易
  • 我们需要 API 来允许用户通过 OTP 使用电话号码登录
  • 我们需要一个前端界面，包括图表、最近交易的列表等。

技术堆栈

选择您选择的堆栈，最初根本不重要。

只需确保您可以轻松托管项目，将其添加到您的简历中，并向仅使用链接的人员展示它们。

我会选择的技术栈

  • 谷歌支付API
  • 在前端做出反应
  • 用于托管的 Vercel
  • 用于版本控制的 GitHub 随意做任何您想做的事情，因为这与堆栈无关。

结论

  • 使用您最喜欢的语言和技术堆栈开发项目
  • 创建前端接口以通过 OTP 使用电话号码登录
  • 创建 API 或方法来发送 OTP 并允许登录
  • 使用 google pay API 获取用户的电话号码交易。
  • 显示炫酷的图表和所有交易的列表。
  • 在 GitHub 上开源该项目并在社交媒体上分享
  • 在示例 URL 上部署项目并在简历上添加链接这样，您的第一个项目就可以添加到简历中了，所以请尝试一下，让我知道您是否遇到任何问题。

继续发展Shrey

我讨厌阅读

Develop it, share it, get the next job and thank me later

Under the Hood

I won’t say much but did you read my last LinkedIn post?

LinkedIn post on learn programming

Now the point is simple, I’ve told multiple times to a lot of developers how to grab your job, how to learn programming, how to become a top-notch developer and bla bla

The question is simple do we need to learn DSA, do we need to master some top Udemy courses or do we need to make a resume from Chat GPT to stand out?

Write a Program to Learn Programming

It’s not rocket science anymore, we need to write programs to learn programming.

We need to develop projects to become good developers or even experienced.

Experience developers are not experienced because of age but because they have extensive experience in developing a variety of projects.

Or Experienced developers are the ones who have mastery of developing one thing at a time but almost to the level of perfectionism.

So you have no other options left even if Chat GPT arrives in the next few years, developers still have to write programs to become good developers.

Small Project to Start

So since we have to understand what to do so now is the time to know where to start.

I am giving one project idea, the real-world website project, this is a full-stack project so the choice is yours on which tech-stack you want to choose.

Google Pay Expense Manager

In India, 80–90% of transactions are done via online payment called UPI mostly by the middle class and above class people.

It’s not available in other countries yet but India is way ahead and other countries like France, and Japan are copying the same model.

Online transactions are easy and effective and less prone to errors.

Now since most of the transactions are online, managing expenses can be made easy for people.

The project is to develop

Online google pay expense manager

  • The user will be logged in via the phone number on the website to connect to google pay
  • Users can see the last 100 or 200 or 3/4 months' transactions.
  • The website should categorise the transactions enabling easy to understand for the user about all of his/her expenses
  • More filter options along with real-time charts for better analysis can be given. Simple yet effective projects, try to make it as good-looking as possible.

My advice is to use the simple black-and-white combination to make it appealing.

Refer to this for the sample design
Sample design

Plan of Action

  • We need Google API to fetch the user transactions
  • We need API to allow user to logged in using the phone number via OTP
  • We need a frontend interface including charts, a list of the last transactions and so on.

Tech Stack

Choose the stack of your choice it doesn’t matter initially at all.

Just make sure you can easily host the project to add them to your resume and showcase them to the people just using the link.

Tech stack I will choose

  • Google Pay API
  • Firebase for phone authentication
  • React in frontend
  • Next.js react framework
  • Node.JS for backend API
  • MongoDB as the database
  • Vercel for hosting
  • Tailwind CSS for styling
  • Twilio as SMS sending API
  • GitHub for version control Feel free to do whatever you want to do as it doesn’t matter much about the stack.

Conclusion

  • Develop the project using your fav language and tech stack
  • Create a frontend interface to log in using a phone number via OTP
  • Create API or method to send OTP and allow logged in
  • Fetch the user's phone number transactions using google pay API.
  • Show cool charts and a list of all the transactions.
  • Open source the project on GitHub and share it on social media
  • Deploy the project on the sample URL and add the link on the resume In this way, your first project is ready to add to the resume, so do give it a try and lemme know if you face any problems.

Keep developing Shrey

Items.Title: 使用 CSS 进行砖石布局 | Masonry Layout with CSS
Items.Description:
有一次我正在探索 HTML 内容的不同类型的布局，我发现了砌体网格。 我第一次在 Pinterest 上看到它，它看起来非常酷且流畅，就像一款图像俄罗斯方块游戏。 我开始研究如何做到这一点，这不会那么困难，对吧？ 嗯，不完全是。

什么是砌体？

砌体布局是一种网格布局，其中项目沿内联方向放置。 当它们换行到下一行时，这些项目将自动填充第一行项目留下的任何空白。 这与当今 CSS 网格的工作方式形成鲜明对比，其中第二行项目不会渗透到第一行中。

如今的 CSS 网格解决方案无法将项目动态放置在前一行留下的空间中，至少在这个过程中不涉及一定量的 JavaScript。 因此，我向您提供上述所有问题的解决方案； 一个办法

虽然该属性主要用于排版和文本内容，但它也可以用于以砖石方式布置图像或卡片。

 .masonry { columns: 3; } .grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); }

然而，这种方法有一些注意事项。 使用网格，项目沿内联方向放置。 然而，对于列，项目被放置在块方向上。 它看起来像砖石布局，但其功能并不完全像砖石布局。 请记住，砌体布局仍然应该在内联方向上布局； 从左到右。

结论

正如你所看到的，我们已经可以非常接近使用纯 CSS 的砖石布局，如果你只是喜欢砖石布局的俄罗斯方块性质的外观，你很可能只使用 CSS 列并像以前一样快乐。 但如果您依赖于真实砖石布局的内联流程，那么您现在只需要涉及一些 JavaScript。

然而，正在制定计划让CSS 原生支持砌体布局。 目前该规范仅在 Firefox 中受支持，但我们只能希望其他浏览器尽快加入砌体列车。



原文:

One time I was exploring different kinds of layouts for HTML content, I found out about the masonry grid. I first saw it on Pinterest, and it looked so cool and fluid, like a game of Tetris for images. I started researching how it could be done, it couldn't be that difficult, right? CSS was powerful enough to make this work, right? Well, not quite.

What is masonry?

Masonry layout is a type of grid layout where the items are placed out in the inline direction. When they wrap to the next line, the items will automatically fill in any gaps left by the first row of items. This is in stark contrast to how CSS grids work today, where the second row of items does not bleed into the first.

Todays CSS grid solution doesn't have the ability to dynamically place items in the spaces left by the previous row, at least not without some amount of JavaScript involved in the process. Thus, I present to you the solution to all of the aforementioned problems; CSS columns.

A solution

CSS columns have been around for a good while now. And while the property has mostly been used for typography and textual content, it can just as well be used for laying out images or cards in masonry-ish way.

.masonry { columns: 3;
} .grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
}

There are some caveats to this method however. Using grid, the items are placed in the inline direction. With columns however, the items are placed in the block direction. It looks like a masonry layout, but it doesn't quite function like one. Remember, masonry layouts are still supposed to be laid out in the inline direction; from left to right.

Conclusion

As you can see, we can already get quite close to masonry layouts with pure CSS, and if you just like the look of the Tetris-ish nature of the masonry layout, you can most likely just use CSS columns and be as happy as ever. But if you are dependant on the inline flow of a real masonry layout, you are just going to have to get some JavaScript involved for now.

There are, however, plans in motion to get masonry layouts natively supported in CSS. The spec is only supported in Firefox at the moment, but we can only hope the other browsers jump on the masonry-train soon enough.

Items.Title: 如何将 appsettings.json 添加到 Blazor WebAssembly 项目 | How can you add appsettings.json to a Blazor WebAssembly project
Items.Description:
在大多数 .Net 项目中，使用提供的模板创建新项目时，蝙蝠右侧会包含两个文件，这两个文件是appsettings.json和appsettings.development.json ，但是，当对于 Blazor WebAssembly，默认的 blazor 模板不提供 appsettings.json，但是，如果您需要一个文件来存储稍后可能要从中加载的一些配置，您可以轻松添加它，让我们看看如何操作。

重要的提示

请仔细阅读此说明，因为它非常重要。

您之前可能使用过appsettings.json来存储数据库连接字符串、密码和敏感信息，但是当涉及 B​​lazor WebAssembly 时，不要这样做，不要在该文件中放入任何敏感数据，为什么？
好吧，因为如果您运行该项目，并通过开发人员工具转到浏览器，则可以检查该文件，并且您肯定不希望您的连接字符串或数据库密码完全暴露，挂在那里非常正确，所以再次强调，不要在其中放置秘密信息，我将向您展示如何从浏览器访问此文件。

我有一个 Blazor WebAssembly 项目，其中有一个 appsettings.json 文件，如果我打开开发人员工具，无论我使用哪个浏览器（在本例中为 Microsoft Edge），然后导航到网络选项卡，我都会看到 appsettings.json实际上已加载到客户端

如果我单击它，它将显示其内容，因此，您不得将任何想要隐藏的数据放入该文件中。

现在让我们学习如何添加appsettings.json

要做到这一点，非常轻松且简单，您可以按照以下基本步骤来完成。
就是这样，您现在有了一个可以从中加载配置的文件，让我们尝试从中提取一些信息，并将它们显示在页面上。

以下代码段适用于 appsettings.json：

 { "Message": "Hello From appsettings.json", "User": { "Id": "123", "Username": "RasheedMozaffar" } }

现在我们可以将IConfiguration实例注入到我们想要访问配置的任何页面中，然后像在任何其他 .Net 项目中一样开始使用它。

让我们在Index.razor中测试一下：

 @page "/" @inject IConfiguration configuration <PageTitle>Index</PageTitle> <h1>Hello, world!</h1> @configuration["Message"] <br/> Welcome to your new app. <SurveyPrompt Title="How is Blazor working for you?" />

如果我运行该应用程序，我应该能够看到如下内容：

现在让我们做一些更有趣的事情，并从配置文件中提取整个部分，我们可以通过创建一个映射到 appsettings 中的User对象的类来做到这一点，并从那里获取值以创建User类型的实例，以下代码适用于User类：

 public class User { public string Id {get; set;} public string Username {get; set;} }

修改Index.razor以创建一个新实例，当页面初始化时，实例属性将设置为从 appsettings.json 中提取的值，如下所示：

 @code{ private User user = new(); protected override void OnInitialized() { user = configuration.GetSection("User").Get<User>()!; } }

现在我们可以添加一些 html 标记来在页面上显示信息：

 <h1>Hello, world!</h1> @configuration["Message"] <br /> <br /> <br /> <h3>User details</h3> <ul> <li>@user.Id</li> <li>@user.Username</li> </ul> Welcome to your new app. <SurveyPrompt Title="How is Blazor working for you?" />

现在您已经知道了，这就是如何将 appsettings.json 文件添加到 Blazor Wasm 项目中，我们还研究了如何打印出存储在键中的值，以及如何加载整个部分并将其值存储在一个 C# 对象，我希望你学到了一些新东西！

现在再见👋🏻



原文:

In most .Net projects, there're two files that are included right of the bat when creating a new project using the provided templates, and these two files are appsettings.json and appsettings.development.json, however, that's not the case when it comes to Blazor WebAssembly, appsettings.json is not provided by the default blazor templates, but, you can easily add it if you need a file to store some configurations that you may want to load from it later, let's see how.

IMPORTANT NOTE

Please read this note carefully, as it's really really imporant.

You may have used appsettings.json before to store your database connection strings, passwords and sensitive information, but when it comes to Blazor WebAssembly, don't do that, do not put any sensitive data in that file, why?
Well, because if you run the project, and go to the browser, through the developer tools, the file can be inspected, and for sure you don't want your connection string or database password to be fully exposed, hanging out there in the wild right, so again, don't put secretive pieces of information in there, I'll show you how we can access this file from the browser.

I have a Blazor WebAssembly project that has an appsettings.json file, If I open the developer tools, whichever browser I'm rocking (Microsoft Edge in this instance), and navigate to the network tab, I'll see that appsettings.json was in fact loaded to the client

If I click on it, its content will be displayed, and for that reason, you MUST NOT put any piece of data that you want to remain hidden, inside of this file.

Let's learn how to add appsettings.json now

To do that, it's pretty effortless and simple, you can do it by following the upcoming basic steps.
1: Right click on wwwroot
2: Click add new item
3: Select app settings file
4: Call it appsettings.json
5: Enjoy

That's it, you now have a file to load configuration from, let's try pulling some information from it, and display them on a page.

The following snippet is for appsettings.json:


{ "Message": "Hello From appsettings.json", "User": { "Id": "123", "Username": "RasheedMozaffar" }
}

Now we can inject an IConfiguration instance into whichever page we want to access the configurations from, and just start using it like we do in any other .Net project.

Let's test it in Index.razor:


@page "/"
@inject IConfiguration configuration <PageTitle>Index</PageTitle> <h1>Hello, world!</h1> @configuration["Message"] <br/> Welcome to your new app. <SurveyPrompt Title="How is Blazor working for you?" />

If I run the app, I should be able to see something like this:

Let's now do something more interesting, and pull an entire section from the config file, we can do that by creating a class that maps to the User object we have in appsettings, and get the values from there to create an instance of type User, the following code is for the User class:


public class User
{ public string Id {get; set;} public string Username {get; set;}
}

Modify Index.razor to create a new instance, and when the page is initialized, the instance properties are set to those values pulled from appsettings.json, like this:


@code{ private User user = new(); protected override void OnInitialized() { user = configuration.GetSection("User").Get<User>()!; }
}

Now we can add some html markup to display the information on the page:


<h1>Hello, world!</h1> @configuration["Message"]
<br /> <br /> <br /> <h3>User details</h3>
<ul> <li>@user.Id</li> <li>@user.Username</li>
</ul> Welcome to your new app. <SurveyPrompt Title="How is Blazor working for you?" />

And there you have it, that's how you can add an appsettings.json file to your Blazor Wasm projects, also we looked at how to print out the values stored in a key, and how we can load an entire section and store its values in an C# object, I hope you learned something new!

Bye now 👋🏻

Items.Title: Widget 之旅：了解 Flutter 中的生命周期 | The Journey of a Widget: Understanding the Lifecycle in Flutter
Items.Description:
在 Flutter 中，小部件有一个生命周期，决定它们如何创建、更新和处置。 了解 widget 生命周期对于管理状态、执行副作用和优化 Flutter 应用程序至关重要。 让我们深入了解 Flutter 中 widget 生命周期的概述：

建造

当您通过调用小部件的构造函数来实例化小部件时，就会创建该小部件。 在构建过程中，您通常会提供任何必需的参数并初始化小部件的状态。 对于小部件的每个实例，构造函数都会被调用一次。

初始化

构建完成后，框架会调用小部件相应 State 对象的 initState() 方法。 您可以在此处执行任何需要小部件访问其 BuildContext 的初始化任务。 您可以初始化变量、订阅流、设置侦听器以及执行其他设置任务。

建造

每当需要渲染或更新小部件时都会调用 build() 方法。 此方法返回一个小部件树，根据小部件的当前状态描述 UI 的外观。 框架最初会调用 build() ，随后每当小部件的状态发生变化或其父级请求重建时，都会调用 build() 。

状态更新

当小部件的状态通过用户交互、数据更改或其他事件发生变化时，框架会调用小部件 State 对象的 setState() 方法。 这会触发小部件的重建，导致框架再次调用 build()。 通过调用 setState()，您可以发出信号，表明小部件的状态已更改并且应反映在 UI 中。

重建

当框架确定需要重建某个小部件时，它会调用 build() 方法来获取新的小部件树。 将新树与之前的树进行比较，框架对 UI 进行必要的更新以反映更改。 重建过程非常高效，因为 Flutter 使用比较算法来确定更新 UI 所需的最小更改。

停用

如果某个小部件已从小部件树中删除，但稍后可能会添加回来，则框架将调用该小部件的 State 对象的 deactivate() 方法。 此方法允许小部件清理不再需要的任何资源，同时保留其状态。 例如，您可以暂停动画、取消订阅或释放资源。

处理

当一个小部件从小部件树中永久删除时，框架会调用该小部件的 State 对象的 dispose() 方法。 这是小部件释放资源、取消订阅或执行任何清理任务的最后机会。 调用 dispose() 后，小部件及其状态不再可用。

需要注意的是，Flutter 中的 widget 是不可变的。 当 widget 的状态发生变化时，Flutter 会重建 widget 树，创建一组具有更新状态的新 widget。 这种方法可以实现高效的 UI 更新，并有助于保持声明式编程风格。

了解 widget 生命周期使您能够适当地管理状态、处理异步操作，并通过最大限度地减少不必要的重建和资源使用来优化 Flutter 应用程序的性能。

了解这些周期对于管理状态和控制小部件的行为至关重要。 让我们探讨一下每个周期：

  • 它保存该小部件的所有可变状态。 通过将 Mounted 属性设置为 true，State 对象与 BuildContext 关联。

  • 它仅在第一次创建状态对象时执行一次。 使用此方法来初始化变量和订阅数据源。

  • 当小部件依赖的对象发生变化时也会调用它。 使用此方法来处理依赖项的更改，但很少需要它，因为构建方法总是在此之后调用。

  • 它在 didChangeDependency() 之后以及每当需要重建小部件时调用。 在此方法中更新小部件的 UI。

  • 它接收旧的小部件作为参数，允许您将其与新的小部件进行比较。 使用此方法来处理小部件配置中的更改。

  • 每当您修改状态时，请使用此方法来触发小部件 UI 的重建。

  • 使用此方法进行任何清理或暂停正在进行的操作。

  • 使用此方法来清理资源，例如数据侦听器或关闭连接。

在dispose()方法之后，State对象不再在树中，并且mounted属性被设置为false。 状态对象无法重新安装。

了解这些生命周期有助于管理状态更改、处理依赖关系以及在有状态小部件中有效更新 UI。

注意：构造函数不是生命周期的一部分，因为在此期间小部件属性的状态为空。

现在您已经全面了解了 Flutter 中的 widget 生命周期，您可以利用其强大功能来创建健壮且高效的 Flutter 应用程序。



原文:

In Flutter, widgets have a lifecycle that determines how they are created, updated, and disposed of. Understanding the widget lifecycle is essential for managing state, performing side effects, and optimizing your Flutter application. Let’s dive into an overview of the widget lifecycle in Flutter:

Construction

When you instantiate a widget by calling its constructor, the widget is created. During construction, you typically provide any required parameters and initialize the widget’s state. The constructor is called once for each instance of the widget.

Initialization

After construction, the framework calls the initState() method of the widget's corresponding State object. This is where you can perform any initialization tasks that require the widget to have access to its BuildContext. You can initialize variables, subscribe to streams, set up listeners, and perform other setup tasks.

Build

The build() method is called whenever the widget needs to be rendered or updated. This method returns a widget tree, describing how the UI should look based on the current state of the widget. The framework invokes build() initially and subsequently whenever the widget's state changes or when its parent requests a rebuild.

State Updates

When the widget’s state changes, either through user interaction, data changes, or other events, the framework calls the setState() method of the widget's State object. This triggers a rebuild of the widget, causing the framework to call build() again. By calling setState(), you signal that the widget's state has changed and should be reflected in the UI.

Rebuilding

When the framework determines that a widget needs to be rebuilt, it calls the build() method to obtain a new widget tree. The new tree is compared to the previous tree, and the framework applies the necessary updates to the UI to reflect the changes. The rebuild process is efficient because Flutter uses a diffing algorithm to determine the minimal changes required to update the UI.

Deactivation

If a widget is removed from the widget tree but might be added back later, the framework calls the deactivate() method of the widget's State object. This method allows the widget to clean up any resources it no longer needs while preserving its state. For example, you can pause animations, cancel subscriptions, or release resources.

Disposal

When a widget is removed from the widget tree permanently, the framework calls the dispose() method of the widget's State object. This is the final opportunity for the widget to release resources, cancel subscriptions, or perform any cleanup tasks. After dispose() is called, the widget and its state are no longer usable.

It’s important to note that widgets in Flutter are immutable. When a widget’s state changes, Flutter rebuilds the widget tree, creating a new set of widgets with the updated state. This approach allows for efficient UI updates and helps maintain a declarative programming style.

Understanding the widget lifecycle enables you to manage state appropriately, handle asynchronous operations, and optimize your Flutter app’s performance by minimizing unnecessary rebuilds and resource usage.

The lifecycle of a stateful widget in Flutter consists of seven cycles. Understanding these cycles is essential for managing the state and controlling the behavior of the widget. Let’s explore each cycle:

  • createState(): This method is required and creates a State object for the widget. It holds all the mutable state for that widget. The State object is associated with the BuildContext by setting the mounted property to true.

  • initState(): This method is automatically called after the widget is inserted into the tree. It is executed only once when the state object is created for the first time. Use this method for initializing variables and subscribing to data sources.

  • didChangeDependencies(): The framework calls this method immediately after initState(). It is also called when an object that the widget depends on changes. Use this method to handle changes in dependencies, but it is rarely needed as the build method is always called after this.

  • build(): This method is required and is called many times during the lifecycle. It is called after didChangeDependencies() and whenever the widget needs to be rebuilt. Update the UI of the widget in this method.

  • didUpdateWidget(): This method is called when the parent widget changes its configuration and requires the widget to rebuild. It receives the old widget as an argument, allowing you to compare it with the new widget. Use this method to handle changes in the widget's configuration.

  • setState(): The setState() method notifies the framework that the internal state of the widget has changed and needs to be updated. Whenever you modify the state, use this method to trigger a rebuild of the widget's UI.

  • deactivate(): This method is called when the widget is removed from the widget tree but can be reinserted before the current frame changes are finished. Use this method for any cleanup or pausing ongoing operations.

  • dispose(): This method is called when the State object is permanently removed from the widget tree. Use this method for cleaning up resources, such as data listeners or closing connections.

After the dispose() method, the State object is no longer in the tree, and the mounted property is set to false. The state object cannot be remounted.

Understanding these lifecycle cycles helps in managing state changes, handling dependencies, and updating the UI effectively in stateful widgets.

Note: The constructor function is not part of the lifecycle as the state of the widget property is empty during that time.

Now that you have a comprehensive understanding of the widget lifecycle in Flutter, you can harness its power to create robust and efficient Flutter applications.

Items.Title: ReasonReact、Auth0 和第 3 方 React 组件 | ReasonReact, Auth0 and 3rd Party React Components
Items.Description:
我喜欢测试技术的方法之一是尝试并实现现实应用程序中可能需要的东西，而身份验证就是其中之一。 我也一直想看看使用第三方 JS React 组件与ReasonReact是多么容易。 因此，为了一石二鸟，我创建了一个使用Auth0 React 组件的简单应用程序。 您可以在此处查看该应用程序，代码位于此处。

使用Auth0Provider组件

应用程序的登录基于 React 的Auth0 快速入门教程，我们要做的第一件事是将我们的App包装在Auth0Provider中。 在 JavaScript 中，我们会import { Auth0Provider } from '@auth0/auth0-react'; 并使用导入的Auth0Provider组件包装我们的App组件，但在 ReasonReact 中，我们需要使用Melange 属性编写一些与组件的绑定。

 module Auth0Provider = { type auth_param = {redirect_uri: string}; [@bs.module "@auth0/auth0-react"] [@react.component] external make: ( ~domain: string, ~clientId: string, ~authorizationParams: auth_param, ~children: React.element ) => React.element = "Auth0Provider"; };

末尾的= "Auth0Provider"是我们要绑定的值/组件的 JavaScript 名称。 两者之间的所有内容都是 ReasonReact 中 React 组件的类型签名。

在 Reason 中，每个文件都是一个模块，通常每个文件都有一个 ReasonReact 组件，文件名就是组件的名称。 我们还可以在文件中定义模块，这就是我们在上面的代码片段中为Auth0Provider所做的事情，并且我们使用该组件，如下所示：

 <Auth0Provider domain="dev-dj37pygvjwvaxjde.us.auth0.com" clientId="e6mvq0WBov5jSBW0clTFdAIXgbyyK4gv" authorizationParams={redirect_uri: origin ++ "/profile"}> <App /> </Auth0Provider>

使用useAuth0()挂钩

现在我们已经用Auth0Provider包装了我们的App ，我们可以开始在我们的组件中使用useAuth0()钩子，为此，我们必须编写一些到该钩子的绑定。

因为在 JavaScript 中我们要import钩子，所以我们必须再次使用@bs.module属性来绑定它：

 type hook; [@bs.module "@auth0/auth0-react"] external useAuth0: unit => hook = "useAuth0"; |--------------------1-------------------| |---2---| |-3-| |-4-| |----5----|

在这里，我们告诉 Reason，我们正在绑定到模块"@auth0/auth0-react" (1) 中的外部内容，并且该内容在 JS 领域称为"useAuth0" (5)； 我们将"useAuth0" (5) 绑定到 Reason land 中的名称useAuth0 (2)； 我们必须给返回值一个类型（ type hook ），以便类型系统知道我们刚刚“导入”/绑定到什么，以及当我们想要绑定到钩子的值和方法时我们需要什么。

然后，您只需在 ReasonReact 代码中使用useAuth0Reason即可。 事实上，如果绑定与 Reason 中的保留关键字冲突，您可能需要重命名绑定。）

要调用方法，我们使用bs.send属性：

 [@bs.send] external loginWithRedirect: hook => unit = "loginWithRedirect";

要获取值，我们使用bs.get属性：

 type user; [@bs.get] external user: hook => user = "user";

定义后，我们可以在 ReasonReact 组件中使用绑定：

 // LoginButton.re [@react.component] let make = () => { let ua = Bindings.Auth0.useAuth0(); <button className="py-2 px-4 rounded-md bg-blue-500 text-white" onClick={_ => Bindings.Auth0.loginWithRedirect(ua)}> {React.string("Log In")} </button>; };

可以根据需要编写绑定，一旦编写完毕，完成 Auth0 快速入门指南就非常简单了。

结论

第三方 JS React 组件可以在任何 ReasonReact 项目中成功使用。 绑定可能看起来很复杂，但是一旦您编写了几个绑定，您很快就会掌握它的窍门。

封面图片由Miguel Á 提供。 来自 Pexels 的 Padriñán



原文:

One of the ways I like to test a technology is to try and implement something that could be required in a real world app, and authentication is one of those things. I have also been wanting to see how easy it is to use a third-party JS React component with ReasonReact. So, to kill two birds with one stone I created a simple app that uses the Auth0 React component. You can view the app here, and the code is here.

Using the Auth0Provider component

The login for the app is based on the Auth0 quick start tutorial for React, and the first thing we have to do is wrap our App in the Auth0Provider. In JavaScript we would do import { Auth0Provider } from '@auth0/auth0-react'; and wrap our App component with the imported Auth0Provider component, but in ReasonReact we need to write some bindings to the component using Melange attributes.

module Auth0Provider = { type auth_param = {redirect_uri: string}; [@bs.module "@auth0/auth0-react"] [@react.component] external make: ( ~domain: string, ~clientId: string, ~authorizationParams: auth_param, ~children: React.element ) => React.element = "Auth0Provider";
};

@bs.module is a Melange attribute that lets us bind to a value from a JS module. The = "Auth0Provider" at the end is the JavaScript name of the value/component we are binding to. The external before the make is used for JS interop/FFI and is like an FFI let binding. Everything else in between is the type signature of a React component in ReasonReact.

In Reason each file is a module, and it is common to have one ReasonReact component per file with the filename being the name of the component. We can also define modules within files which is what we have done for the Auth0Provider in the snippet above, and we use the component like so:

<Auth0Provider domain="dev-dj37pygvjwvaxjde.us.auth0.com" clientId="e6mvq0WBov5jSBW0clTFdAIXgbyyK4gv" authorizationParams={redirect_uri: origin ++ "/profile"}> <App />
</Auth0Provider>

Using the useAuth0() hook

Now that we have wrapped our App with the Auth0Provider we can start using the useAuth0() hook in our components, and to do so we must write some bindings to the hook.

Because in JavaScript we would import the hook we again have to use the @bs.module attribute to bind to it:

type hook;
[@bs.module "@auth0/auth0-react"] external useAuth0: unit => hook = "useAuth0";
|--------------------1-------------------| |---2---| |-3-| |-4-| |----5----|

Here we are telling Reason that we are binding to an external something in the module "@auth0/auth0-react"(1) and that something is called "useAuth0"(5) in JS land; we are binding "useAuth0"(5) to the name useAuth0(2) in Reason land; and useAuth(2) takes a unit(3) (i.e., takes no arguments; equivalent to useAuth0() in JS) and returns a hook(4). We have to give the return value a type (type hook) so that the type system knows what we have just "imported"/bound to, and which we need when we want to bind to the values and methods of the hook.

(You don't have to use the same JS name in Reason for a binding. For example, you could do something like:

[@bs.module "@auth0/auth0-react"] external useAuth0Reason: unit => hook = "useAuth0";

You would then just use useAuth0Reason in your ReasonReact code. In fact, you may need to rename bindings if they clash with reserved key words in Reason.)

The useAuth0 hook provides various state values and methods, and to use them we need more bindings.

To call a method we use the bs.send attribute:

[@bs.send] external loginWithRedirect: hook => unit = "loginWithRedirect";

To get a value we use the bs.get attribute:

type user;
[@bs.get] external user: hook => user = "user";

Once defined we can use the bindings in our ReasonReact components:

// LoginButton.re
[@react.component]
let make = () => { let ua = Bindings.Auth0.useAuth0(); <button className="py-2 px-4 rounded-md bg-blue-500 text-white" onClick={_ => Bindings.Auth0.loginWithRedirect(ua)}> {React.string("Log In")} </button>;
};

And the very readable output JavaScript of the LoginButton ReasonReact component:

// Generated by Melange import * as React from "react";
import * as Auth0React from "@auth0/auth0-react"; function LoginButton(Props) { var ua = Auth0React.useAuth0(); return React.createElement( "button", { className: "py-2 px-4 rounded-md bg-blue-500 text-white", onClick: function (param) { ua.loginWithRedirect(); }, }, "Log In" );
} var make = LoginButton; export { make };
/* react Not a pure module */

Bindings can be written as they are needed, and once written completing the Auth0 quick start guide is pretty straight forward.

Conclusion

Third-party JS React components can be successfully used in any ReasonReact project. Bindings may look complicated, but once you've written a couple of them you quickly get the hang of it.

Items.Title: 将 AWS CDK 部署速度提高高达 80% | Speed up AWS CDK deploys up to 80%
Items.Description:
请阅读Medium 上的这篇文章来支持我。

介绍

当我们在终端中运行 cdk 命令时，npm 包在运行之前使用 ts-node 包来转译 CDK 代码。

问题

我发现运行cdk deploy或cdk synth命令时需要“永远”。 当我进行故障排除或开发代码时，我想要更快的部署。 我在开发过程中使用cdk watch命令来热交换更改。 这快了“一点”，但仍然感觉超级慢。 我想找出为什么这些 CDK 命令花费这么长时间。

这就是解决方案

我将预先分享解决方案。 帖子的其余部分将分享我的发现。

我使用 npm 安装了一个新的 ts-node 转换器。

 npm i -D @swc/core @swc/helpers regenerator-runtime

我更新了tsconfig.json文件以告诉 ts-node 使用新的 SWC 转换器。 我在其下添加了ts-node属性和swc属性。

 { "ts-node": { "swc": true } }

在终端中运行cdk和npx ts-node命令时，我注意到有了显着的改进。

请记住SWC 网站上的这条注释：

因此，建议您继续使用 tsc 来检测任何类型错误。

我是如何找到解决方案的

我意识到 ts-node 可能是问题所在。 我使用 TypeScript 编写命令行脚本。 我使用npx ts-node myfile.ts命令来执行它们。 他们花了一段时间来执行，但并没有想太多。 当我对 CDK 命令启动时间感到沮丧时，我开始意识到 TypeScript 脚本执行时间也很慢。

我决定调查它们是否是 ts-node 包的速度问题。

我发现了一个 GitHub 问题，其他人表达了他们对 ts-node 缓慢的担忧。

讨论让我了解了 SWC 转译器，它可以加快 ts-node 的使用时间。

我安装了 SWC 软件包并更新了tsconfig.json文件。 我注意到在终端中使用cdk deploy 、 cdk synth 、 cdk watch和npx ts-node命令时有显着的改进。 现在开发时间快多了。

我更新了 CI/CD 管道，该管道部署多个 CDK 应用程序并运行各种 TypeScript 命令行脚本。 添加 ts-node SWC 设置后，我的 CI/CD 管道执行时间从 345 秒下降到 74 秒。 哇！ 超过 75%。

我需要与 CDK 社区分享这一发现。 我提交了 GitHub Pull 请求，将 SWC 设置添加到 TypeScript 应用模板。

如果拉取请求获得批准并合并，由 cdk init 命令创建的新 CDK 应用程序将获得此速度提升。

实证检验

我们来做一些测试，看看 SWC 编译器的效果。

让我们为 CDK 应用程序创建一个文件夹。 我们将使用 ~/cdkapps 文件夹。 注意：我的设置使用 Docker ，而不是直接将 Node.js 安装到我的计算机上。

第一次测试

我们将创建一个新的 CDK 应用程序。

 cd ~/cdkapps mkdir test1-cdk cd test1-cdk npx cdk init app --language typescript

让我们测试一下合成一个空应用程序所需的时间。

该命令发送了以下输出：

 real 0m38.681s user 0m46.195s sys 0m1.769s

合成一个 CDK 应用程序花了 46 秒。

现在让我们使用 SWC 转译器进行测试。 该命令发送了以下输出：

 real 0m9.562s user 0m9.105s sys 0m0.703s

使用 SWC 时，合成 CDK 应用程序需要 9 秒。 提升了 80%！

第二次测试

现在，让我们使用一个 TypeScript Lambda 函数创建一个新的 CDK 应用程序。

我们将创建一个新的 CDK 应用程序。

 cd ~/cdkapps mkdir test2-cdk cd test2-cdk npx cdk init app --language typescript

让我们添加包来帮助添加 Lambda 函数。

 npm i -D @types/aws-lambda

让我们创建 Lambda 函数。

让我们创建函数代码。

 /* ~/cdkapps/test2-cdk/function.ts */ import { Handler } from 'aws-lambda'; export const handler: Handler<Event> = async function(event, _context) { return; }

让我们创建函数资源。

 /* ~/cdkapps/test2-cdk/lib/test2-cdk-stack.ts */ import * as cdk from 'aws-cdk-lib'; import { Construct } from 'constructs'; import * as path from 'path'; export class Test2CdkStack extends cdk.Stack { constructor(scope: Construct, id: string, props?: cdk.StackProps) { super(scope, id, props); new cdk.aws_lambda_nodejs.NodejsFunction(this, 'function', { functionName: 'function', handler: 'handler', runtime: cdk.aws_lambda.Runtime.NODEJS_18_X, entry: path.resolve('__dirname', '..', 'function.ts'), }); } }

让我们测试一下在裸 TypeScript Lambda 函数上合成应用程序所需的时间。

该命令发送了以下输出：

 real 0m49.314s user 0m58.874s sys 0m3.818s

合成一个 CDK 应用程序花了 58 秒。

现在让我们使用 SWC 转译器进行测试。 该命令发送了以下输出：

 real 0m18.985s user 0m18.625s sys 0m2.186s

使用 SWC 时，合成 CDK 应用程序需要 18 秒。 提升了 67%。

结论

考虑将 ts-node 的 SWC 转译器添加到您的 AWS CDK 应用程序。 时间结果将根据您的设置而有所不同。

你走之前

以下是您可能会喜欢的其他帖子。

照片由 Unsplash 上的 PAUL SMITH 拍摄



原文:

The ts-node package needed the speed boost

Support me by reading this post on Medium.

Introduction

The Amazon Web Services Cloud Development Kit is an infrastructure-as-code solution. CDK allows us to write code (in JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, for example) to programmatically deploy resources in our AWS account.

CDK is written in TypeScript and uses the ts-node package to deploy. When we run the cdk command in the terminal, the npm package uses the ts-node package to transpile the CDK code before running it.

The issue

I was finding it was taking “forever” when running a cdk deploy or cdk synth command. When I was troubleshooting or developing code, I wanted faster deploys. I was using the cdk watch command to hotswap changes during development. This was a “little” faster but still felt super slow. I wanted to find out why these CDK commands were taking so long.

This was the solution

I will share the solution upfront. The rest of the post will share my findings.

I installed a new ts-node transpiler using npm.

npm i -D @swc/core @swc/helpers regenerator-runtime

I updated my tsconfig.json file to tell ts-node to use the new SWC transpiler. I added the ts-node property and the swc property under it.

{ "ts-node": { "swc": true }
}

I noticed a significant improvement when running cdk and npx ts-node commands in the terminal.

Keep in mind this note from the SWC website:

SWC only transpiles the code and doesn’t perform type checking. Therefore, it’s recommended that you continue to use tsc for detecting any type errors.

How I found the solution

I realized that ts-node could be the issue. I write command line scripts with TypeScript. I use the npx ts-node myfile.ts command to execute them. They took a while to execute but did not think much about it. When I was getting frustrated by the CDK command start times, I started to realize the TypeScript script execution time was slow too.

I decided to investigate if they were speed issues with the ts-node package.

I found a GitHub issue where others expressed their concerns with the slowness of ts-node.

github.com/TypeStrong/ts-node/issues/10…

The discussion taught me about the SWC transpiler that can speed up the time ts-node uses.

typestrong.org/ts-node/docs/swc/

I installed the SWC packages and updated my tsconfig.json file. I noticed a significant improvement when using cdk deploy, cdk synth, cdk watch, and npx ts-node commands in the terminal. Development time was much faster now.

I updated my CI/CD pipeline that deploys multiple CDK apps and runs various TypeScript command line scripts. My CI/CD pipeline execution time dropped from 345 seconds to 74 seconds when I added the ts-node SWC setting. Wow! That’s over 75%.

I needed to share this finding with the CDK community. I submitted a GitHub pull request to add the SWC settings to the TypeScript app template.

github.com/aws/aws-cdk/pull/25089

If the pull request is approved and merged, new CDK apps created by the cdk init command will get this speed boost.

Empirical testing

Let’s do some testing to see the effects of the SWC transpiler.

Let’s create a folder for our CDK apps. We will use the ~/cdkapps folder. Note: My setup uses Docker rather than installing Node.js directly onto my machine.

The first test

We will create a new CDK app.

cd ~/cdkapps
mkdir test1-cdk
cd test1-cdk
npx cdk init app --language typescript

Let’s test the time it takes to synthesize an empty app.

The command sent this output:

real 0m38.681s
user 0m46.195s
sys 0m1.769s

It took 46 seconds to synthesize a CDK app.

Now let’s test with the SWC transpiler. (The command assumes the steps to add the SWC transpiler were already done.)

The command sent this output:

real 0m9.562s
user 0m9.105s
sys 0m0.703s

It took 9 seconds to synthesize a CDK app when using SWC. That’s an 80% improvement!

The second test

Now let’s create a new CDK app with one TypeScript Lambda function.

We will create a new CDK app.

cd ~/cdkapps
mkdir test2-cdk
cd test2-cdk
npx cdk init app --language typescript

Let’s add the packages to help with adding a Lambda function.

npm i -D @types/aws-lambda

Let’s create the Lambda function.

Let’s create the function code.

/* ~/cdkapps/test2-cdk/function.ts */
import { Handler } from 'aws-lambda'; export const handler: Handler<Event> = async function(event, _context) { return;
}

Let’s create the function resource.

/* ~/cdkapps/test2-cdk/lib/test2-cdk-stack.ts */
import * as cdk from 'aws-cdk-lib';
import { Construct } from 'constructs';
import * as path from 'path'; export class Test2CdkStack extends cdk.Stack { constructor(scope: Construct, id: string, props?: cdk.StackProps) { super(scope, id, props); new cdk.aws_lambda_nodejs.NodejsFunction(this, 'function', { functionName: 'function', handler: 'handler', runtime: cdk.aws_lambda.Runtime.NODEJS_18_X, entry: path.resolve('__dirname', '..', 'function.ts'), }); }
}

Let’s test the time it takes to synthesize an app on a bare TypeScript Lambda function.

The command sent this output:

real 0m49.314s
user 0m58.874s
sys 0m3.818s

It took 58 seconds to synthesize a CDK app.

Now let’s test with the SWC transpiler. (The command assumes the steps to add the SWC transpiler were already done.)

The command sent this output:

real 0m18.985s
user 0m18.625s
sys 0m2.186s

It took 18 seconds to synthesize a CDK app when using SWC. That’s a 67% improvement.

Conclusion

Consider adding the SWC transpiler for ts-node to your AWS CDK apps. The time results will vary depending on your setup.

Before you go

Here are other posts you might enjoy.

AWS CDK Serverless Cookbook: A Step-by-step Guide to Build a Serverless App in the Cloud

Stop Installing Node.js and Global Npm Packages, Use Docker Instead: Protect your system from vulnerabilities

Cloudflare v. AWS: A Comparison of Their Serverless Offerings: They both have their benefits and downsides

How I Caused an Amazon API Gateway Denial of Service: The DoS attack was caused by a Lambda function

Items.Title: 微服务中的 API 延迟 – 基于跟踪的故障排除 | API latency in microservices – Trace-based troubleshooting
Items.Description:
原文： gethelios.dev/blog/api-latency-in-micro...

在微服务架构中，应用程序被分解为小型的独立服务，这些服务使用 API 以同步或异步方式相互通信。

微服务具有许多优点，例如更高的灵活性和可扩展性（微服务可以彼此独立地扩展，并且 API 通过根据需要添加或删除服务实例来帮助扩展微服务）、增强的可靠性（其中一个故障不会影响微服务）。其他微服务如整体应用程序）、更好的安全性、减少的开发时间等等。

然而，微服务 API 也并非没有问题。 它是微服务应用程序性能的关键因素。

在这方面，澄清延迟和响应时间之间的区别很重要。 这是衡量系统性能的两个重要指标。 它们经常被混淆，但实际上它们是两个不同的东西。 响应时间是系统响应请求所花费的时间。 它包括系统接收请求、处理请求和生成响应所花费的时间。 潜伏。 另一方面，是消息从一个点传输到另一个点所需的时间。 它包括消息通过网络传输所花费的时间，以及系统接收和处理消息所花费的时间。 简而言之，就是远程响应时间。 虽然两者都应该进行优化，但本文着眼于延迟。

毫不奇怪，延迟是微服务中的一个大问题，因为微服务应用程序更多地依赖于 API 之间的通信：在单体应用程序中，所有代码和数据都位于单个进程中。 这当然可以更轻松地快速响应请求。 然而，在微服务应用程序中，每个服务都是其自己的单独进程。 这意味着事务事件必须在进程之间、不同的内部 API 之间传输，这会增加响应时间并可能导致高延迟。 扩展微服务时，问题会变得更加严重——随着服务复杂性的增加，微服务和应用程序编程接口 (API) 之间的延迟存在增加的重大风险

微服务与整体响应时间

来源

为什么会出现这样的问题呢？ 由于多种原因，延迟成为一个问题，例如用户响应时间慢、服务器负载增加、可扩展性降低等等。

此外，微服务中的 API 延迟调试和故障排除具有挑战性，因为追踪根本原因可能是开发人员的噩梦。 毕竟，它可能是由单个服务引起的，也可能与多个服务之间的通信有关。

本文讨论了微服务中延迟的挑战以及减少延迟的一些策略、最佳实践和示例。

微服务中的内部 API 性能问题
微服务架构中的 API 可能会出现许多性能故障或问题。 一些最常见的问题包括：

延迟：如上所述，延迟是 API 响应请求所需的时间。

当请求或响应出现问题时，API 将返回 HTTP 错误。 一些常见的示例包括 404 Not Found、500 Internal Server Error 和 403 Forbidden。

超时：当延迟太高并且 API 响应请求的时间太长时，可能会发生超时。 超时可能是由多种因素引起的，例如网络拥塞、服务器过载或代码错误。

连接错误：当 API 无法连接到服务器时，可能会发生连接错误。 连接错误可能由多种因素引起，例如网络问题、服务器中断或防火墙规则。

无效数据：可以在请求中向 API 发送无效数据。 无效数据可能会导致 API 中出现错误，例如格式错误的请求或无效参数。

深入研究微服务中的 API 延迟
微服务中的 API 延迟定义为 API 接收请求、处理请求和生成响应所需的时间。

微服务的数量：架构中的微服务越多，潜在的延迟点就越多（因此在扩展微服务时问题就更大）

微服务的复杂性：微服务越复杂，处理请求所需的时间就越多。

网络基础设施：网络基础设施的质量也会影响延迟。

网络延迟：消息从一个点传输到另一个点所需的时间可能是延迟的主要因素。 这可能是由距离、网络拥塞和数据包丢失等因素引起的。

服务器延迟：服务器处理请求所需的时间也会导致延迟。 这可能是由服务器负载、服务器资源和请求的复杂性等因素引起的。

数据库延迟：数据库返回数据所需的时间也可能是延迟的一个因素。 这可能是由数据库大小、数据库负载和查询复杂性等因素引起的。

例如，如果 API 的设计不高效，则可能需要客户端和服务器之间进行更多往返，这会增加延迟。

缓存：不使用缓存可能会导致 API 在每次发出请求时从较慢的存储介质（例如数据库）获取数据。

负载均衡：如果负载均衡没有正确实现，请求可能会被路由到过载的服务器。

服务网格：如果不使用服务网格，就没有管理和监控微服务的中心点。

可观察性：在微服务中，如果没有正确观察延迟，可能会导致性能问题和中断。 传统的监控方法无法解决微服务架构中的 API 延迟问题，因为它们并非旨在处理此类架构的复杂性。 他们通常专注于监控单个服务器，并且仅靠他们无法通过分布式架构连接整个客户旅程中的依赖关系和性能。

虽然监控很重要，但云原生应用程序架构需要可观察性。 正如我们关于 API 可观察性与监控的文章中详细介绍的那样，与监控相比，API 可观察性通过观察各个服务及其之间的依赖关系，可以在一处实时查看更广泛的性能数据。 它使用日志、指标和跟踪来创建整体监控策略。

有效排除 API 延迟问题
要解决微服务架构中的 API 延迟问题，使用专为此类架构设计的可观测性解决方案非常重要。 这些解决方案通常提供以下功能：

分布式跟踪：分布式跟踪允许您查看请求通过微服务架构所经过的路径。 这可以帮助您识别延迟问题的根源。

服务级别目标 (SLO)：SLO 允许您定义微服务可接受的性能级别。 这可以帮助您在延迟问题影响用户之前识别并解决延迟问题。

警报：警报允许您在微服务架构中出现延迟问题时收到通知。 这可以帮助您在问题影响用户之前快速识别和解决问题。

使用分布式跟踪和 OpenTelemtry 调试 API 延迟
分布式跟踪是一种跟踪请求通过分布式系统的方式，以便识别和解决性能瓶颈和其他问题。 实现分布式跟踪的最强大工具是 OpenTelemetry，它是一个 OSS 可观察性框架，可通过数据检测从各​​种来源（包括应用程序、服务和基础设施）收集和导出遥测数据。 该数据可用于识别性能瓶颈和其他问题。 它是一个很棒的工具，因为它与供应商无关，并且不依赖于任何特定的供应商、技术、语言或框架。

毫无疑问，OTel 是一个改变生活的工具，但它有一些缺点，可以通过使用基于此 OSS 的第三方工具来解决。 主要问题包括实施和维护复杂性、缺乏后端存储、缺乏可视化层以及基于收集的数据没有可操作的见解。 为了有效地对微服务中的分布式系统和 API 延迟进行故障排除，开发人员需要一种端到端的可观测性解决方案，该解决方案能够可视化跟踪和跨度，并收集精细的错误和性能相关数据。

它可以帮助开发人员立即安装和维护 OpenTelemetry、收集完整的有效负载数据、存储遥测数据、可视化跟踪和跨度、将它们与日志和指标关联起来，并启用错误洞察和警报。

该工具为目录中的每个特定 API 提供了一个仪表板。 这包括最近跨度的趋势、持续时间分布、HTTP 响应状态代码、错误和失败等。 它使开发人员可以利用每个 API 调用的完整 E2E 上下文按错误过滤 API，从而使他们能够利用最相关的上下文和流程驱动思维来调查发生的情况。

示例：API 延迟增加的根本原因分析
本文中也分享了这个示例：API 可观测性：利用 OTel 改善开发者体验。

在此示例中，显示可视化的流程由各种端点组成，并涉及多个服务。

这是应用程序中可视化 API 的第一个入口点：

根据检测数据检查延迟最大的 API 调用

使用 API 概述来分析客户报告的延迟并对其进行故障排除 - 通过快速识别异常值长跨度

专注于代表延迟增加的 API 调用和跨度

调查继续针对跟踪的最小子集，通过单击其可视化并深入了解详细信息（通过查明瓶颈的持续时间功能）来完成：

调查其他跨度可以揭示趋势，并查看问题是否出现在所有其他跟踪中以及所有跟踪中是否都存在瓶颈。

结论
总之，API 延迟对微服务架构构成了重大挑战。 它可能是由多种因素造成的，包括微服务的数量、复杂性、网络基础设施和 API 的设计。

为了解决 API 延迟问题，使用专为微服务架构设计的可观测性解决方案非常重要。 然而，并非所有可观测性解决方案都提供解决微服务架构中 API 延迟问题所需的所有功能。

例如，某些解决方案不会从架构中的所有微服务收集数据。 其他人不提供基于跟踪的可视化，这可能导致难以识别延迟问题的根源。 其他人仍然不提供错误见解和警报，这可能导致难以采取行动解决延迟问题。

为了充分利用 OpenTelemetry，您应该使用包含可视化、有效负载的精细数据、见解和错误警报的解决方案。

Original article: gethelios.dev/blog/api-latency-in-micro…

In microservices architectures, apps are broken down into small, independent services that communicate with each other using APIs in a synchronous or asynchronous way.

Microservices carry many advantages, such as Increased flexibility and scalability (microservices can be scaled independently of each other, and APIs help to scale microservices by adding or removing instances of the service as needed), enhanced reliability (a failure in one doesn’t effect the other microservices as in monolith apps), better security, reduced development time and more.

However, microservices APIs are not problem free. API latency is a major obstacle in microservices, and it’s defined as the time it takes for an API to respond to a request. It is a critical factor in the performance of microservices applications.

In this aspect, it’s important to clarify the difference between latency and response time. These are two important metrics for measuring the performance of a system. They are often confused with each other, but they are actually two different things. Response time is the time it takes for a system to respond to a request. It includes the time it takes for the system to receive the request, process the request, and generate a response. Latency. On the other hand, is the time it takes for a message to travel from one point to another. It includes the time it takes for the message to travel through the network, as well as the time it takes for the system to receive and process the message. In short, it’s the remote response time. While both should be optimized, this article looks at latency.

Not surprisingly, latency is a big problem in microservices as microservices applications depend more on the communication between APIs: In a monolithic application, all of the code and data is located in a single process. This of course makes it easier to respond to requests quickly. In a microservices application, however, each service is its own individual process. This means that transaction events have to travel between processes, across different internal APIs which adds to the response time and may cause high latency. The problem worsens when scaling microservices – As the complexity of the service increases, there is a significant risk of increasing latency between microservices and application programming interfaces (APIs)

Microservices vs monolith response time

Source

Why is it such a problem? Latency is a problem due to multiple reasons, such as slow response time for users, increased load on servers, reduced scalability, and more.

Moreover, debugging and troubleshooting API latency in microservices is challenging as tracking down the root cause can be a developer nightmare. After all, it can be caused by one single service, or it can be related to the communication between multiple services.

This article discusses the challenges of latency in microservices as well as some strategies, best practices and examples for reducing latency.

Internal APIs performance issues in microservices
There are a number of performance failures or issues that can occur with APIs in microservices architectures. Some of the most common issues include:

Latency: As mentioned, latency is the time it takes for an API to respond to a request.

HTTP errors: HTTP errors are a common type of error that can occur with APIs. HTTP errors are returned by the API when there is a problem with the request or the response. Some common examples include 404 Not Found, 500 Internal Server Error, and 403 Forbidden.

Timeouts: Timeouts can occur when the latency is just too high and an API takes too long to respond to a request. Timeouts can be caused by a variety of factors, such as network congestion, overloaded servers, or errors in the code.

Connection errors: Connection errors can occur when an API is unable to connect to the server. Connection errors can be caused by a variety of factors, such as network problems, server outages, or firewall rules.

Invalid data: Invalid data can be sent to an API in a request. Invalid data can cause errors in the API, such as malformed requests or invalid parameters.

Deep dive into API latency in microservices
API latency in microservices is defined as the time it takes for the API to receive the request, process the request, and generate a response.

High API latency can occur due to multiple reasons, including:

The number of microservices: The more microservices there are in an architecture, the more potential points of latency there are (therefore the problem is bigger when scaling microservices)

The complexity of the microservices: The more complex the microservices are, the more time it takes for them to process requests.

The network infrastructure: The quality of the network infrastructure can also impact latency.

Network latency: The time it takes for a message to travel from one point to another can be a major factor in latency. This can be caused by factors such as distance, network congestion, and packet loss.

Server latency: The time it takes for a server to process a request can also contribute to latency. This can be caused by factors such as server load, server resources, and the complexity of the request.

Database latency: The time it takes for a database to return data can also be a factor in latency. This can be caused by factors such as database size, database load, and the complexity of the query.

API design: The design of the API can also impact latency. For example, if the API is not designed to be efficient, it may require more round trips between the client and the server, which can increase latency.

Caching: Not using caching can cause the API to fetch data from a slower storage medium, such as a database, every time a request is made.

Load balancing: If load balancing is not implemented correctly, requests may be routed to overloaded servers.

Service mesh: If a service mesh is not used, there will be no central point for managing and monitoring microservices.

Observability: in microservices, If latency is not properly observed, it can lead to performance problems and outages. ** Troubleshooting high API latency in microservices** Why do traditional monitoring methods fail?

Traditional monitoring methods fail to troubleshoot API latency in microservices architectures because they were not designed to handle the complexity of such architectures. They typically focus on monitoring individual servers and they alone can’t connect dependencies and performance across the entire customer journey through distributed architecture.

While monitoring is important, cloud-native application architectures require observability instead. As detailed in our article about API observability versus monitoring, API observability views much wider performance data compared to monitoring, in one place and in real-time, through observing individual services and the dependencies between them. It uses logs, metrics, and tracing to create a holistic strategy for monitoring.

Troubleshooting API latency effectively
To troubleshoot API latency in microservices architectures, it is important to use an observability solution that is designed for this type of architecture. These solutions typically provide features such as:

Distributed tracing: Distributed tracing allows you to see the path that a request takes through a microservices architecture. This can help you to identify the source of latency problems.

Service level objectives (SLOs): SLOs allow you to define acceptable levels of performance for your microservices. This can help you to identify and troubleshoot latency problems before they impact your users.

Alerting: Alerting allows you to be notified when there are latency problems in your microservices architecture. This can help you to quickly identify and troubleshoot problems before they impact your users.

Debugging API latency with distributed tracing and OpenTelemtry
Distributed tracing is a way of tracking requests as they travel through a distributed system in order to identify and resolve performance bottlenecks and other problems. The most powerful tool to implement distributed tracing is OpenTelemetry, an OSS observability framework that collects and exports telemetry data from a variety of sources including apps, services, and infrastructure, through data instrumentation. OpenTelemetry can be used to collect data about the request, including the time it took to complete. This data can be used to identify performance bottlenecks and other issues. It’s a great tool as it’s vendor-neutral, and not tied to any specific vendor, technology, language or framework.

While no doubt that OTel is a life-changing tool, it has a few disadvantages that can be dealt with by using 3rd party tools that are based on this OSS. The main issues include implementation and maintenance complexity, lack of backend storage, lack of a visualization layer, and no actionable insights based on the data it collects. ** E2E trace-based observability: Visualization and error insights**

For effective troubleshooting of distributed systems and API latency in microservices, in particular, developers need an E2E observability solution that visualizes traces and spans, as well as collects granular error and performance-related data.

Helios is an OTel-based tool that helps Dev and Ops teams minimize MTTR in distributed applications. It helps developers install and maintain OpenTelemetry in no time, collect the full payload data, store telemetry data, visualize traces and spans, correlate them with logs and metrics, and enable error insights and alerts.

The tool provides a dashboard for each specific API in the catalog. This includes trends of the recent spans, duration distribution, HTTP response status code, errors and failures, and more. It lets developers filter APIs by errors with the full E2E context of each API call, enabling them to investigate what happened with the most relevant context and flow-drive mindset.

An example: Root cause analysis of the increase in API latency
This example is also shared in this article: API observability: Leveraging OTel to improve developer experience.

In this example, the flow showing the visualization is composed of various endpoints and involves several services.

Here’s the first entry point of the visualization’s API in the app:

Inspecting the API calls with the largest latency based on instrumented data

Using the API overview for analyzing & troubleshooting latency reported by customers – by quickly identifying the outlier long spans

Zeroing in on the API calls and spans that represent the increased latency

The investigation continues towards a minimal subset of traces, done by clicking on their visualizations and drilling down into the details (through the duration feature that pinpoints the bottlenecks):

Analyzing bottlenecks in the E2E flow using trace visualization

Investigating other spans can reveal a trend and see if the issue occurs in all other traces and if bottlenecks exist in all of them.

Conclusion
In conclusion, API latency poses a major challenge to microservice architectures. It can be caused by a variety of factors, including the number of microservices, their complexity, the network infrastructure, and the design of the API.

To troubleshoot API latency, it is important to use an observability solution that is specifically designed for microservices architectures. However, not all observability solutions provide all of the features that are needed to troubleshoot API latency in microservice architectures.

For example, some solutions do not collect data from all of the microservices in an architecture. Others do not provide trace-based visualization, which can make it difficult to identify the source of latency problems. Others still do not provide error insights and alerts, which can make it difficult to take action to resolve latency problems.

In order to make the most out of OpenTelemetry, you should use a solution that includes both visualization, granular data of the payloads, insights and error alerts.

*About Helios
*
Helios is a dev-first observability platform that helps Dev and Ops teams shorten the time to find and fix issues in distributed applications. Built on OpenTelemetry, Helios provides traces and correlates them with logs and metrics, enabling end-to-end app visibility and faster troubleshooting.

Items.Title: 希腊论证：古代艺术应用于现代软件测试 | Greek Argumentation: The Ancient Art Applied to Modern Software Testing
Items.Description:
您是否想过古希腊哲学与软件测试可能有什么共同点？ 听起来很有趣，对吧？ 系好安全带，开启一段令人惊奇的旅程，将软件 QA 与苏格拉底、亚里士多德及其同时代人联系起来。 但不要害怕！ 我向你保证，我们不会在这里争论现实的本质或美德的含义。 我们将借用一些已经存在了数千年的想法，看看它们如何在数字世界中为我们提供帮助。

为什么要用希腊论证？

希腊论证是由古希腊哲学家和修辞学家发展起来的逻辑和辩论体系，以强调推理、系统提问和有效沟通而闻名。 乍一看，它与软件测试的距离就像奥林匹斯山距离硅谷一样遥远。 但如果我们仔细观察，就会发现这两个领域都需要结构化思维、怀疑心态和有效沟通的能力。

逻辑和推理：测试的支柱

我们的第一站是逻辑和推理原理。 这两者是软件测试的核心，构成了系统方法的支柱，用于查找程序在何处以及在什么条件下可能会失败。 这个希腊概念教会我们从结构上思考软件，类似于建筑师检查建筑物的方式。

苏格拉底方法：质疑一切！

接下来，我们来看看苏格拉底最喜欢的工具，苏格拉底方法。 当应用于软件测试时，它提醒我们质疑一切。 这些探测查询可以帮助揭示软件需求中隐藏的假设、模糊性或不一致之处。 这就像在黑暗的房间里打开手电筒一样 - 突然，您看到了您甚至不知道的东西！

演绎推理：从规范到测试用例

亚里士多德的演绎推理思想，从一般原理到具体结论，完美地映射到生成测试用例的过程。 如果系统应该具有某种特征（一般原则），那么软件应该在特定条件下以某种方式运行（特定结论）。

修辞的力量：沟通是关键

修辞学是说服的艺术，是希腊思想家的另一个创意。 对于软件测试人员来说，良好的沟通技巧与编写代码或设计测试用例的能力一样重要。 无论您是向开发人员解释复杂的错误，向经理展示测试结果，还是说明为什么应该分配更多资源用于测试，修辞艺术都是您坚定的盟友。

诡辩与怀疑：期待意外之事

古代诡辩家以其辩论技巧而闻名（并经常受到批评），他们可以启发软件测试人员预测软件如何以意想不到的方式失败。 这种健康的怀疑态度，这种对意外情况的准备，通常意味着在测试中发现错误或在实际产品中出现错误之间的区别。

辩证法：辩论与学习

最后，辩证法为我们提供了一种结构化的方法来解决开发人员和测试人员之间经常出现的讨论和分歧。 这并不是要证明对方是错误的，而是要综合一种新的观点来调和双方的观点。 这是关于互相学习并最终构建更好的产品。

包起来

这样，您就可以在测试套件中一睹古希腊的风采了。 借鉴希腊论证的精华可以为您的软件测试过程注入新的视角，提供经得起时间考验的强大的推理、提问和沟通方法。

最后，这不是关于古希腊或孤立的软件测试，而是关于解决问题和寻求真相，无论是在哲学辩论中还是在调试讨厌的软件问题时。 这难道不值得我们吸取教训吗？

下次请和我一起继续探索软件测试领域中意想不到的联系。 如果您喜欢这段从古希腊到现代代码的旅程，请不要忘记点赞、分享和评论！ 告诉我您在测试过程中还发现了哪些其他令人惊讶的联系。



原文:

Have you ever thought about what ancient Greek philosophy might have in common with software testing? Sounds intriguing, right? Strap in for a surprising journey connecting software QA to Socrates, Aristotle, and their contemporaries. But fear not! I promise you, we won't be debating the nature of reality or the meaning of virtue here. We'll just borrow some ideas that have survived millennia and see how they can help us in the digital world.

Why Greek Argumentation?

Greek argumentation, a system of logic and debate developed by philosophers and rhetoricians of ancient Greece, is known for its emphasis on reasoning, systematic questioning, and effective communication. At first glance, it might seem as distant from software testing as Mount Olympus from Silicon Valley. But if we look closer, we see both fields require structured thinking, a skeptical mindset, and the ability to communicate effectively.

Logic and Reasoning: The Backbone of Testing

Our first stop is the principle of logic and reasoning. These two are at the core of software testing, forming the backbone of a systematic approach to finding where and under what conditions a program might fail. This Greek concept teaches us to think structurally about the software, similar to how an architect would inspect a building.

The Socratic Method: Question Everything!

Next, we meet Socrates' favorite tool, the Socratic method. When applied to software testing, it reminds us to question everything. These probing queries can help expose hidden assumptions, ambiguities, or inconsistencies in software requirements. It's like turning on a flashlight in a dark room – suddenly, you see things that you didn't even know were there!

Deductive Reasoning: From Specifications to Test Cases

Aristotle's idea of deductive reasoning, moving from general principles to specific conclusions, maps perfectly onto the process of generating test cases. If the system is supposed to have a certain feature (general principle), the software should behave in a certain way under specific conditions (specific conclusion).

The Power of Rhetoric: Communication is Key

Rhetoric is the art of persuasion, another brainchild of Greek thinkers. Good communication skills can be as important for a software tester as the ability to write code or design a test case. Whether you're explaining a complex bug to a developer, presenting test results to a manager, or making a case for why more resources should be allocated to testing, the art of rhetoric is your steadfast ally.

Sophism and Skepticism: Expect the Unexpected

The ancient sophists, renowned (and often criticized) for their argumentative skills, can inspire software testers to anticipate how software might fail in unexpected ways. This healthy skepticism, this preparedness for the unexpected, can often mean the difference between catching a bug in testing or having it show up in a live product.

Dialectic: Debate and Learn

Finally, the dialectic method offers us a structured approach to discussions and disagreements that often arise between developers and testers. This isn't about proving the other side wrong, but rather about synthesizing a new perspective that reconciles both views. It's about learning from each other and ultimately building a better product.

Wrapping Up

So there you have it, a glimpse of ancient Greece in your testing suite. Drawing from the well of Greek argumentation can infuse your software testing processes with a fresh perspective, offering robust methods of reasoning, questioning, and communication that have withstood the test of time.

In the end, it's not about ancient Greece or software testing in isolation, it's about problem-solving and seeking the truth, be it in a philosophical debate or while debugging a pesky software issue. And isn't that a lesson worth learning?

Join me next time as we continue to explore unexpected connections in the world of software testing. If you enjoyed this journey from ancient Greece to modern code, don't forget to like, share, and comment! Tell me what other surprising connections you've found in your testing journey.

提示示例：

总结由三个反引号分隔的文本。

This is some text to summarize.

输出示例：

 "This is a summary of the text."

概括：
分隔符有助于定义和包含提示的各个部分，从而增强清晰度和特异性。

提示示例：

 "Generate a list of two book titles with authors and genres in JSON format with keys: book_id, title, author, genre."

输出示例：

 "[{ "book_id": 1, "title": "Fictional Book", "author": "Fictional Author", "genre": "Fiction" }, { "book_id": 2, "title": "Fictional Book 2", "author": "Fictional Author 2", "genre": "Non-fiction" } ]"

概括：
请求结构化输出使模型能够生成清晰、组织良好的响应。

提示示例：

输出示例：

 "Step 1 - First, do this. Step 2 - Then, do that."

概括：
提示模型识别特定信息并重新格式化它有助于获得满足某些条件的响应。

输入示例：

 "<child>: Teach me about patience. <grandparent>: Patience is a virtue. <child>: Teach me about kindness."

输出示例：

 "<grandparent>: Kindness is the act of being considerate, compassionate, and empathetic towards others."

概括：
在提出最终查询之前提供一些成功的示例有助于指导模型以特定的风格或格式进行响应。

给模型时间思考

  1. 指定完成任务所需的步骤
    1. 要求以指定格式输出
  2. 指示模型在急于得出结论之前找出自己的解决方案

型号限制

减少幻觉

先查找相关信息，然后根据相关信息回答问题

我使用这些指南的第一个提示：

写一条消息在 Twitter 上分享我正在做的这门精彩课程： learn.deeplearning.ai/chatgpt-prompt-en…

请在消息中包含课程learn.deeplearning.ai/chatgpt-prompt-en ... 的链接。

请确保消息在 Twitter 大小限制内。

请确保内容突出显示关键字“提示工程”。

请确保它包含可以使推文流行的相关主题标签。

请确保它涉及一些号召性用语，以便阅读的用户可以对推文采取一些行动。

输出格式如下：

鸣叫：

标签：

原文:

 

Example prompt:

Summarize the text delimited by triple backticks.

This is some text to summarize.

Example output:

"This is a summary of the text."

Summary:
Delimiters help define and contain sections of the prompt, enhancing clarity and specificity.

2. Ask for structured output

Example Prompt:

"Generate a list of two book titles with authors
and genres in JSON format with keys:
book_id, title, author, genre."

Example output:

"[{
    "book_id": 1,
    "title": "Fictional Book",
    "author": "Fictional Author",
    "genre": "Fiction"
  },
  {
    "book_id": 2,
    "title": "Fictional Book 2",
    "author": "Fictional Author 2",
    "genre": "Non-fiction"
  }
]"

Summary:
Requesting structured output allows the model to generate a clear, well-organized response.

3. Check whether conditions are satisfied

Example prompt:

"Rewrite these instructions in step format if present:

First, do this. Then, do that.


Example output:

"Step 1 - First, do this.
Step 2 - Then, do that."

Summary:
Prompting the model to identify specific information and reformat it aids in getting responses that fulfill certain conditions.

4. Few-shot prompting - Give successful examples

Example Input:

"<child>: Teach me about patience.
<grandparent>: Patience is a virtue.
<child>: Teach me about kindness."

Example output:

"<grandparent>: Kindness is the act of being considerate, compassionate, and empathetic towards others."

Summary:
Providing a few successful examples before asking the final query helps guide the model to respond in a particular style or format.

Give the model time to think

  1. Specify the steps required to complete a task
    1. Ask for output in a specified format
  2. Instruct the model to work out its own solution before rushing to a conclusion

Model Limitations

Reducing hallucinations

First find relevant information, then answer the questions based on the relevant information

Write a message to share on Twitter about this fantastic course I am doing: learn.deeplearning.ai/chatgpt-prompt-en…

Please make sure the message is within the Twitter size limit.

Please make sure the content highlights the keyword "prompt engineering".

Please make sure it contains the relevant hashtags that can make the tweet popular.

Please make sure it involves some call to action so users reading can take some action on the tweet.

Output in the following format:

Tweet:

Hashtags:

Items.Title: 通过 GitHub API 获取贡献者的 5 种不同方式 | 5 Different Ways to Get Contributors through the GitHub API
Items.Description:
您是否曾经考虑过获取有关某些 Git 项目的信息并创建仪表板来分析它们？ 该博客将通过向您提供此类项目的初始部分来帮助您，即获取项目的贡献者。 那么让我们开始吧：

本博客使用 GitHub API 探索从 GitHub 项目检索贡献者信息的各种方法。 请记住，API 仅显示与 GitHub 用户名关联的前 500 个贡献者，其余的显示为匿名贡献者。

首先，让我们检查 GitHub API 文档以了解可用信息和端点：

端点提供有关项目贡献者的信息。 我们将使用可选标头来最大限度地减少 API 请求。

以下代码提供了一种获取贡献者的简单方法。 为了代码简单起见，我们将该过程分为两个步骤。 首先，我们请求端点，然后检查是否已到达列表的末尾。

 async function getContributors(repoName, page = 1) {   let request = await fetch(`https://api.github.com/repos/${repoName}/contributors?per_page=100&page=${page}`, {    method: 'GET',    headers: {      'Content-Type': 'application/json',    }  });   // print data from the fetch on screen  let contributorsList = await request.json();  return contributorsList; };

在此函数中，我们将存储库名称和页面作为参数传递，并且根据文档将per_page保留为最大值 100。

 per_page is The number of results per page (max 100).

我们现在将编写一个函数来迭代请求并收集所有贡献者。

为此，我们将执行以下操作：

  • 有一个贡献者列表以添加日期。
  • 遍历整个页面并获取贡献者
  • 如果我们得到一个空列表就停止。
  • 返回贡献者列表
async function getAlllContributors(repoName) {  let contributors = [];  let page = 1;  do {    list = await getContributors(repoName, page);    contributors = contributors.concat(list);    page++;  } while (list.length > 0);  // while (list.length%100 !== 0)  return contributors; }

在这种方法中，我们使用了 do-while 循环来在第一个请求时获取贡献者并随之移动。 这种方法将返回我们需要的所有贡献者。

至于 do-while 循环中的条件，我们可以使用另一个条件，即

如果我们这样做，它将返回相同的结果，但我们将控制请求是否返回每页的最大结果。

或者，我们可以递归地编写代码。 此版本继续获取贡献者，直到到达列表末尾。

步骤如下：

  • 获取所有贡献者
  • 如果是列表末尾，则返回整个列表
  • 否则，再次调用该函数。
 async function getAllContributorsRecursive_version1(repoName, page = 1, contributors = []) {   const list = await getContributors(repoName, page);  if (list.length === 0) {    return contributors;  }  contributors = contributors.concat(list);  return getAllContributors(repoName, page + 1, contributors); }

这里的getContributors方法与第一个示例中的方法类似。

我们可以通过执行以下操作以类似的逻辑但使用不同的方法来完成此操作：

  • 获得贡献
  • 如果有列表，则再次调用该函数
  • 如果列表已完成，则返回所有贡献者。
 async function getAllContributorsRecursive(repoName, page = 1, allContributors = []) {  const list = await getContributors(repoName, page);  allContributors = allContributors.concat(list);  if (list.length === 100) {    return getAllContributorsRecursive(repoName, page + 1, allContributors);  }  // The base case: when the list is empty, return allContributors  return allContributors; }

如果您想使用官方 GitHub 包来处理请求，Octokit 会很方便。 您可以通过此链接OctoKit访问 GitHub 存储库。 它为更大的请求提供功能和身份验证选项。 以下是如何使用它来获取贡献者：

 await octokit.request("GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contributors", { owner: "octocat", repo: "hello-world", per_page: 100, page: 1, });

编写前面的代码的另一种方法是使用其余端点，这是该代码的简写，您可以在下一个链接文档中获取文档

async function getContributorsOctoKit(repo, owner, page = 1) {  return request = await octokit.rest.repos.listContributors({    owner,    repo,    per_page: 100,    page  }); }

现在，我们可以以与之前完成的获取类似的方式迭代整个代码，

 async function rest(){  let contributors = [];  let page = 1;  let request;    do {    request = await getContributorsOctoKit("tensorflow", "tensorflow", page);    page++;    contributors = contributors.concat(request.data);  } while (request.data.length > 0);  return contributors; }

最后，Octokit 提供了方便的分页功能，可以轻松迭代 API 响应。 以下是如何使用它来获取贡献者：

 async function paginate(){  let contributors = [];  const iterator = octokit.paginate.iterator(octokit.rest.repos.listContributors, {    owner: "tensorflow",    repo: "tensorflow",    per_page: 100,  });  // iterate through each response  for await (const { data: users } of iterator) {    for (const user of users) {      contributors.push(user);    }  }  return contributors; }

这五种方法提供了使用 GitHub API 检索所有存储库贡献者的各种方法。 您可以使用这些方法来深入了解您的项目并为其他 GitHub REST API 端点自定义它们。 如果您想测试代码示例，可以在此存储库中找到它们。 该存储库已准备好使用 VsCode 进行调试。

最后，请随时关注圣训科技新闻通讯，以获取技术领导者的见解。
